Our Lord Jesus encouraged even the poorest people to come to him for life and grace. Christ knows and considers our circumstances. The kindness of Christ is always available; to show this, he repeated this miracle. His blessings are renewed as our needs and necessities arise. And those need not fear lack, who have Christ to live upon by faith, and do so with thanks.

Stubborn disbelief will have something to say, even if it is unreasonable. Christ refused to answer their demand. If they will not be convinced, they shall not. Sadly, we have reason to mourn for those around us, who destroy themselves and others through their stubborn disbelief and opposition to the gospel! When we forget the works of God, and distrust him, we should strongly rebuke ourselves, as Christ here rebukes his disciples. How is it that we so often misunderstand his meaning, disregard his warnings, and distrust his care?

Here is a blind man brought to Christ by his friends. That showed the faith of those who brought him. If those who are spiritually blind do not pray for themselves, then their friends and relatives should pray for them, that Christ would be pleased to heal them. The cure happened gradually, which was unusual in our Lord’s miracles. Christ showed how people commonly are healed by his grace, who by nature are spiritually blind. At first, their understanding is unclear; but like the light of morning, it shines more and more until a perfect day, and then they see all things clearly. Rejecting Christ's blessings results in losing them; and he will make those who do so know the value of privileges through lacking them.

These things are written that we may believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God. These miracles of our Lord assure us that he was not defeated, but a Conqueror. Now the disciples are convinced that Jesus is the Christ; they can bear to hear about his sufferings, which Christ begins to tell them here. He sees errors in what we say and do that we ourselves are unaware of, and knows what kind of people we are when we ourselves do not. Human wisdom is foolishness when it tries to limit God’s plans. Peter did not rightly understand the nature of Christ's kingdom.

Frequent notice is taken of the large number of people who came to Christ for help in various situations. Everyone is concerned to know this if they expect him to heal their souls. They must not indulge the comfort of the body. As the happiness of heaven with Christ is enough to make up for losing life itself, so gaining all the world through sin will not make up for the ruin of the soul by sin. And there is a day coming when the cause of Christ will appear as glorious as some now think it unimportant and shameful. May we think about that season, and view every earthly object as we shall do at that great day.
